title: Virtual guidance for orthopedic surgical procedures
abstract: An example method includes displaying, via a visualization device and overlaid on a portion of an anatomy of a patient viewable via the visualization device, a virtual model of the portion of the anatomy obtained from a virtual surgical plan for an orthopedic joint repair surgical procedure to attach a prosthetic to the anatomy; and displaying, via the visualization device and overlaid on the portion of the anatomy, a virtual guide that guides at least one of preparation of the anatomy for attachment of the prosthetic or attachment of the prosthetic to the anatomy.
claims: 1. A method comprising: storing, by a computing system, an information model that describes a surgical workflow that comprises a series of steps that a user would perform to complete an orthopedic joint repair surgical procedure; displaying, to the user via a mixed reality (MR) visualization device and overlaid on a portion of an anatomy of a patient viewable via the MR visualization device, virtual guidance to guide placement of a resection guide on a humerus of the patient as part of the orthopedic joint repair surgical procedure, wherein displaying the virtual guidance comprises: displaying a representation of a virtual cutting plane at a location at which a humeral head of the humerus is to be removed as a result of performance of a resection of the humeral head of the humerus using the resection guide, the resection guide including a physical feature that is aligned with the representation of the virtual cutting plane; receiving, by one or more processors of the computing system, information from one or more sensors that enable the one or more processors to determine a position of a saw relative to the anatomy of the patient; determining, by the one or more processors, based on a current step of the series of steps, whether to display or hide a secondary view window; based on a determination to display the secondary view window, displaying, to the user via the MR visualization device, the secondary view window that includes a virtual 3-dimensional (3D) model of the humerus and a virtual 3D model of the saw, wherein a perspective of the secondary view window is different from a perspective of the user and the perspective of the secondary view window is initially a default perspective that is tied to the current step; receiving, by the one or more processors, input from the user to adjust the perspective of the secondary view window; and in response to the input, changing, by the one or more processors, the perspective of the secondary view window.
